***Hotel California*** is the third [studio album](/wiki/Studio_album "Studio album") by American rapper [Tyga](/wiki/Tyga "Tyga"). It was released on April 9, 2013, by [Cash Money Records](/wiki/Cash_Money_Records "Cash Money Records"), [Republic Records](/wiki/Republic_Records "Republic Records") and [Young Money Entertainment](/wiki/Young_Money_Entertainment "Young Money Entertainment"). The album features [guest appearances](/wiki/Guest_appearance "Guest appearance") from [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne"), [2 Chainz](/wiki/2_Chainz "2 Chainz"), [The Game](/wiki/Game_(rapper) "Game (rapper)"), [Jadakiss](/wiki/Jadakiss "Jadakiss"), while the [production](/wiki/Record_production "Record production") on the album was handled by [DJ Mustard](/wiki/DJ_Mustard "DJ Mustard"), [Detail](/wiki/Detail_(musician) "Detail (musician)"), [Cool & Dre](/wiki/Cool_%26_Dre "Cool & Dre"), [David D.A. Doman](/wiki/David_D.A._Doman "David D.A. Doman"), [Mars of 1500 or Nothin'](/wiki/Grand_Hustle_Records#In-house_producers "Grand Hustle Records"), [The Olympicks](/wiki/The_Olympicks "The Olympicks"), [SAP](/wiki/SAP_(producer) "SAP (producer)") and Ryan Hunt, among others.

*Hotel California* was supported by three [singles](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)"): "[Dope](/wiki/Dope_(Tyga_song) "Dope (Tyga song)")" featuring [Rick Ross](/wiki/Rick_Ross "Rick Ross"), "[For the Road](/wiki/For_the_Road "For the Road")" featuring [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), and "[Show You](/wiki/Show_You "Show You")" featuring [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)"); while serving as the promotional single was "[Molly](/wiki/Molly_(Tyga_song) "Molly (Tyga song)")" featuring [Cedric Gervais](/wiki/Cedric_Gervais "Cedric Gervais"), [Wiz Khalifa](/wiki/Wiz_Khalifa "Wiz Khalifa") and Mally Mall.

The album was met with mixed reviews from [music critics](/wiki/Music_criticism "Music criticism"). Commercially, it debuted at number seven on the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), with first-week sales of 54,000 copies in the United States.[[1]](#cite_note-hiphopdx1-1) It was later certified [gold](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA) in March 2020.

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hotel_California_(Tyga_album)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

On August 20, 2012, Tyga announced the title to his third album, which would be called *Hotel California*.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) Tyga explained he made the album in a very short time so all the material would sound the same and go well together. He also compared it to a really good [mixtape](/wiki/Mixtape "Mixtape") saying you wouldn't see much music dedicated to the commercial fans.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The title is a dedication to his home state of [California](/wiki/California "California").[[5]](#cite_note-autogenerated1-5) Most of the album's sound is based on 1990s [West Coast hip hop](/wiki/West_Coast_hip_hop "West Coast hip hop") and sampling.[[6]](#cite_note-autogenerated3-6)

## Recording and production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hotel_California_(Tyga_album)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Recording and production")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2d/Lil_Wayne_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Lil_Wayne_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lil_Wayne_(cropped).jpg)

CEO of [Young Money](/wiki/Young_Money_Entertainment "Young Money Entertainment"), [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne") appeared on the song "500 Degrees", and served as one of the album's [executive producers](/wiki/Executive_producer "Executive producer").

On November 24, during his tour [vlog](/wiki/Vlog "Vlog"), Tyga announced [Rick Ross](/wiki/Rick_Ross "Rick Ross"), [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj"), [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), [Wiz Khalifa](/wiki/Wiz_Khalifa "Wiz Khalifa") and [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne"), to be featured on his upcoming album.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The album was named number 28 on [XXL](/wiki/XXL_(magazine) "XXL (magazine)")'s Most Anticipated Albums of 2013 list.[[8]](#cite_note-8) On February 7, 2013, Tyga said that he'd was almost finished with the album.[[5]](#cite_note-autogenerated1-5) He confirmed that he had worked with a fellow rapper [ASAP Rocky](/wiki/ASAP_Rocky "ASAP Rocky") for this album, but didn't make the final track listing.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Musician [JMSN](/wiki/JMSN "JMSN") was confirmed as one of his multiple collaborations to the album, during his interview with [Complex](/wiki/Complex_(magazine) "Complex (magazine)").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

The album features [guest appearances](/wiki/Guest_appearance "Guest appearance") from [2 Chainz](/wiki/2_Chainz "2 Chainz"), [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)"), [The Game](/wiki/Game_(rapper) "Game (rapper)") and [Jadakiss](/wiki/Jadakiss "Jadakiss").[[11]](#cite_note-autogenerated4-11) The [Tupac](/wiki/Tupac_Shakur "Tupac Shakur") feature on the song was one of the most talked about announcements, with the song sharing the same title as Tupac's 1996 [diss record](/wiki/Diss_record "Diss record"), "[Hit Em Up](/wiki/Hit_Em_Up "Hit Em Up")". Tyga later clarified that this song was not a remake of Tupac's classic record, but would include unreleased Tupac vocals. He intended to have the song feature Nicki Minaj, but he decided to use Jadakiss as the [guest vocalist](/wiki/Guest_appearance "Guest appearance") to sing with the Tupac vocals.[[12]](#cite_note-12) However, Tupac's voice does not appear on the final album version, they could not clear the vocal samples.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Production on the album was handled by mostly lesser known producers, along with [Cool & Dre](/wiki/Cool_%26_Dre "Cool & Dre"), DJ Mustard, [David D.A. Doman](/wiki/David_D.A._Doman "David D.A. Doman"), [Lil' C](/wiki/Lil%27_C_(record_producer) "Lil' C (record producer)"), [SAP](/wiki/SAP_(producer) "SAP (producer)") and [Mars of 1500 or Nothin'](/wiki/Grand_Hustle_Records#In-house_producers "Grand Hustle Records").[[14]](#cite_note-hiphop-n-more1-14)

## Release and promotion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hotel_California_(Tyga_album)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Release and promotion")]

In August 2012, Tyga released the mixtape *[Well Done 3](/wiki/Well_Done_3 "Well Done 3")*, in promotion for the album. The mixtape features guest appearances from The Game, [Honey Cocaine](/wiki/Sochitta_Sal "Sochitta Sal"), 2 Chainz, Future, [Kirko Bangz](/wiki/Kirko_Bangz "Kirko Bangz"), D-Lo and Joe Moses; supporting the mixtape was the single, "[Do My Dance](/wiki/Do_My_Dance "Do My Dance")".[[15]](#cite_note-15) Tyga later announced that he would be releasing a nine-track mixtape under the name *187*.[[16]](#cite_note-16) The mixtape was released on November 30, 2012. It features remixes to songs such as 2 Chainz's "[I'm Different](/wiki/I%27m_Different_(2_Chainz_song) "I'm Different (2 Chainz song)")", [ASAP Rocky](/wiki/ASAP_Rocky "ASAP Rocky")'s "[Fuckin' Problems](/wiki/Fuckin%27_Problems "Fuckin' Problems")" and [GOOD Music](/wiki/GOOD_Music "GOOD Music")'s "[Clique](/wiki/Clique_(song) "Clique (song)")", among others and some new materials.[[17]](#cite_note-187_mixtape_release-17) In January 2013, Tyga announced a *Fan of a Fan 2* mixtape with Chris Brown would be finished when the latter was done touring overseas.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

In November 2012, during a red carpet interview at the [American Music Awards](/wiki/American_Music_Awards "American Music Awards"), he announced that the album would be released for March 2013.[[19]](#cite_note-19) On January 22, 2013, Tyga announced on Twitter that the album would be released on March 26, 2013.[[20]](#cite_note-20) He then pushed it back to April 9, 2013, to avoid clashing with the release of Lil Wayne's *[I Am Not a Human Being II](/wiki/I_Am_Not_a_Human_Being_II "I Am Not a Human Being II")*, and to clear some of the samples he planned to use on the album.[[6]](#cite_note-autogenerated3-6) The release date was revealed by DJ Khaled at the end of "Outro" off the [Cash Money Records](/wiki/Cash_Money_Records "Cash Money Records") mixtape, titled "Rich Gang: All Stars".[[21]](#cite_note-21)

### Tour

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hotel_California_(Tyga_album)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Tour")]

Beginning the first week of April, Tyga began a world tour, in promotion of the album. The tour consists of a month in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") and a month overseas.[[5]](#cite_note-autogenerated1-5)

## Cover artwork

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hotel_California_(Tyga_album)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Cover artwork")]

On March 6, 2013, Tyga released the album cover, along with a trailer for the album. The cover art features Tyga in a white fur coat and hat at a lavish [mansion](/wiki/Mansion "Mansion"), while a tiger sits at his feet.[[22]](#cite_note-autogenerated2-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23) However, it was rumored that he used [photoshop](/wiki/Photoshop "Photoshop") instead of a live tiger.[[24]](#cite_note-24) Tyga later admitted in an interview with [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV") that he jumped the gun by releasing the original cover saying, "Actually that wasn't the official cover, it was never done. I had just tweeted it out like let me see what people think."[[25]](#cite_note-autogenerated5-25) However, the originally-released cover would turn out to be the in store retail cover.[[26]](#cite_note-26) In August 2013, *[Complex](/wiki/Complex_(magazine) "Complex (magazine)")* would name the album cover one of "The 30 Worst Hip-Hop Album Covers of All Time".[[27]](#cite_note-27)

## Singles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hotel_California_(Tyga_album)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Singles")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/80/Rick_Ross_in_2011.jpg/250px-Rick_Ross_in_2011.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rick_Ross_in_2011.jpg)

Rapper [Rick Ross](/wiki/Rick_Ross "Rick Ross") made a guest appearance on the album's first single "[Dope](/wiki/Dope_(Tyga_song) "Dope (Tyga song)")".

In November 2012, Tyga announced that the first single will be titled "[Dope](/wiki/Dope_(Tyga_song) "Dope (Tyga song)")", which features guest vocals from a fellow rapper [Rick Ross](/wiki/Rick_Ross "Rick Ross").[[28]](#cite_note-28) The song was premiered on December 20, 2012, through [Power 106](/wiki/Power_106 "Power 106"). The song features a sample of [Dr. Dre](/wiki/Dr._Dre "Dr. Dre")'s "[Deep Cover](/wiki/Deep_Cover_(song) "Deep Cover (song)")".[[29]](#cite_note-29) The single was released via digital download on January 15, 2013.[[30]](#cite_note-30) The music video was released on January 18, 2013.[[31]](#cite_note-complex.com-31) The song peaked at number 68 on the US [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100").

In February, 2013, Tyga announced that the second single for the album would be titled "[For the Road](/wiki/For_the_Road "For the Road")", which features guest vocals from American recording artist [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown").[[5]](#cite_note-autogenerated1-5) Tyga then said that the song has the same feeling as their hit "[Deuces](/wiki/Deuces_(song) "Deuces (song)")".[[6]](#cite_note-autogenerated3-6) The song was produced by [Cordale "Lil' C" Quinn](/wiki/Lil%27_C_(record_producer) "Lil' C (record producer)"), and it officially premiered on March 25, 2013. The song was released for digital download on April 4, 2013, along with the pre-order for the album.[[32]](#cite_note-32) The music video was filmed in late April, directed by [Colin Tilley](/wiki/Colin_Tilley "Colin Tilley").[[33]](#cite_note-33) The music video for "For the Road" was released on May 19, 2013.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

On July 23, 2013, the music video for "Show You" featuring [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)"), premiered on [106 & Park](/wiki/106_%26_Park "106 & Park").[[35]](#cite_note-35) "Show You" was released to [Rhythmic contemporary](/wiki/Rhythmic_contemporary "Rhythmic contemporary") radio, as the album's third single on August 27, 2013.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

### Promotional singles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hotel_California_(Tyga_album)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Promotional singles")]

On March 15, 2013, Tyga released the previously leaked song, titled "[Molly](/wiki/Molly_(Tyga_song) "Molly (Tyga song)")"; a collaboration with [Wiz Khalifa](/wiki/Wiz_Khalifa "Wiz Khalifa"), Mally Mall and [Cedric Gervais](/wiki/Cedric_Gervais "Cedric Gervais"), as the album's promotional single.[[37]](#cite_note-37) On March 21, 2013, Tyga shot the music video for "Molly" with Wiz Khalifa and Mally Mall. It was shot in downtown Los Angeles and was directed by [Colin Tilley](/wiki/Colin_Tilley "Colin Tilley"). He described the video as [I, Robot](/wiki/I,_Robot_(film) "I, Robot (film)") meets [Alice in Wonderland](/wiki/Alice_in_Wonderland "Alice in Wonderland").[[38]](#cite_note-38) On April 7, 2013, the music video was released.[[39]](#cite_note-39) The song has since peaked at number 66 on the US *Billboard* Hot 100.

### Other songs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hotel_California_(Tyga_album)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Other songs")]

Upon the release, "500 Degrees" featuring [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne"), "Hijack" featuring [2 Chainz](/wiki/2_Chainz "2 Chainz") and "Get Loose" debuted at 38, 39 and 46 respectfully on the *Billboard*'s [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart, respectively.[[40]](#cite_note-40) On September 12, 2013, the music video for "Don’t Hate The Playa" was released.[[41]](#cite_note-41) On September 20, 2013, the music video for "Hijack" featuring 2 Chainz was released.[[42]](#cite_note-42)

*Hotel California* was met with generally mixed reviews from [music critics](/wiki/Music_criticism "Music criticism"). At Metacritic, which assigns a normalized rating out of 100 to reviews from mainstream critics, the album has received an average score of 50, based on 7 reviews, indicating "mixed reviews".[[43]](#cite_note-meta-43) David Jeffries [AllMusic](/wiki/AllMusic "AllMusic") gave the album three out of five stars, saying "Hotel California refuses to sort his over-the-top bangers into anything sensible, and without a "Rack City" to make it crossover worthy, this is a full-length to leave for the fans."[[44]](#cite_note-Allmusic-44) Dave William of *[XXL](/wiki/XXL_(magazine) "XXL (magazine)")* gave the album an M, saying "Tyga still thrives in his comfort zone and there are songs that will find success on this album. It’s a desire for more than that that’s missing here."[[51]](#cite_note-XXL-51) Edwin Ortiz of [HipHopDX](/wiki/HipHopDX "HipHopDX") gave the album two and a half stars out of five, saying "His sexual callousness wears off quickly, and what listeners are left with is a release better fit for a Ramada Inn than a Four Seasons."[[46]](#cite_note-HipHopDX-46) Jeff Weiss of *[Spin](/wiki/Spin_(magazine) "Spin (magazine)")* gave the album a three out of ten, saying "*Hotel California* is inexcusable. It may be the least creative major-label rap album in recent memory. It's like it was A&R'd by the people who green-lit Battleship, the movie."[[50]](#cite_note-spin-50)

## Commercial performance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hotel_California_(Tyga_album)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Commercial performance")]

*Hotel California* debuted at number seven on the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), selling 54,000 copies in its first week.[[1]](#cite_note-hiphopdx1-1) In its second week, the album dropped to number 18 on the chart, selling an additional 19,000 copies.[[52]](#cite_note-52) In its third week, the album dropped to number 30 on the chart, selling 12,000 more copies, bringing its three-week total to 84,000 copies.[[53]](#cite_note-53) On March 19, 2020, the album was certified [gold](/wiki/RIAA_Certification "RIAA Certification") by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA) for combined sales and [album-equivalent units](/wiki/Album-equivalent_unit "Album-equivalent unit") over 500,000 units in the United States.[[54]](#cite_note-54)
